Corporate Structure: Private company / Scale-up (Series E)
Funding Investors: Total funding reported between $2B and $2.4B. Most recent funding was a $350M Series E round. Key investors include Goldman Sachs, T. Rowe Price, Capricorn Investment Group, Eclipse, and NVentures (NVIDIA's venture arm).
Technology Platform: Integrated hydrometallurgical and pyrometallurgical recycling and refining processes to produce battery-grade materials.
Target Materials: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, Copper, and Cathode Active Materials (CAM)
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap and end-of-life (EOL) batteries from electric vehicles and consumer electronics.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations. Two main campuses in the US: Tahoe Campus, Nevada and a Carolina Campus in Charleston, South Carolina. Acquired Redux Recycling in Germany to expand European operations.
Customers Partnerships: Panasonic, GM, Toyota, and Isuzu
Geographic Presence: North America (Nevada, South Carolina) and Europe (via acquisition of Redux in Germany).

Headquarters: Toronto, Ontario, Canada. Now part of Glencore, headquartered in Baar, Switzerland.
Corporate Structure: Public company (NYSE: LICY). Acquired by Glencore Plc in August 2025, operating as a subsidiary.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded. Received strategic investments from Glencore (totaling $275M) and has LG Energy Solution and LG Chem as investors.
Technology Platform: "Spoke & Hub" model. Spokes use mechanical pre-processing to shred batteries and produce "black mass". Hubs use hydrometallurgical processes to refine the black mass into battery-grade materials.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, Copper, Manganese
Waste Stream Focus: Both battery manufacturing scrap and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations at multiple "Spoke" (pre-processing) facilities globally. "Hub" (post-processing) facilities are under development, including a planned site in Sardinia, Italy, in cooperation with Glencore.
Customers Partnerships: Strategic partnership with Glencore, which is also a key investor and offtake partner. Has investment from LG Energy Solution and LG Chem.
Geographic Presence: Global operations with "Spoke" facilities in North America (e.g., Arizona, New York) and Europe. Planned "Hub" facility in Italy.

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed scale-up (Series D).
Funding Investors: Total funding exceeds $1.1 billion. Raised $704 million in 12 months (2023-2024). Key investors include Decarbonization Partners, Temasek, Qatar Investment Authority, Just Climate, Porsche Ventures, and SK Ecoplant. Received grants from the U.S. Department of Energy.
Technology Platform: Direct Recycling using a proprietary "Hydro-to-Cathode®" direct precursor synthesis process.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, Manganese, Graphite. Produces precursor Cathode Active Materials (pCAM), Cathode Active Materials (CAM), and battery-grade lithium carbonate.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap (from gigafactories) and end-of-life batteries (black mass).
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale. Operates a recycled lithium carbonate production facility in Georgia. A large-scale pCAM/CAM manufacturing facility ("Apex 1") is under construction in Hopkinsville, Kentucky, slated to open in 2025.
Customers Partnerships: Has a multi-year, ~$1B pCAM supply contract with an unnamed leading global automaker. Also has a take-or-pay agreement with Trafigura and an MOU for European collaboration with GEM. SK ecoplant is a key investor and partner.
Geographic Presence: North America (facilities in Massachusetts, Georgia, Kentucky) and expanding to Europe (planned facility in Poland).

Corporate Structure: Public company (Euronext Brussels: UMI). An established global materials technology and recycling group.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Funding is through corporate financing. Latest disclosed funding was a grant in July 2022.
Technology Platform: Integrated pyrometallurgical and hydrometallurgical processes in a closed-loop system.
Target Materials: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, Manganese. Achieves >95% recovery for Ni/Co in pilot flowsheets.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap from battery production and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations globally. Operates one of the world's largest multi-metal recycling facilities in Hoboken, Belgium. Expanding hydro- and pyrometallurgical capacity, aiming for industrialization by 2026-2027. Has a CAM plant in Nysa, Poland and plans for a pCAM/CAM site in Ontario, Canada.
Customers Partnerships: Has a joint venture (Ionway) with Volkswagen's PowerCo. Supplies major automotive OEMs and battery manufacturers globally.
Geographic Presence: Global presence with major manufacturing and R&D hubs in Europe (Belgium, Germany, Poland, Finland), North America (USA, Canada), and Asia (China, South Korea).

Corporate Structure: Subsidiary of Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited (CATL).
Funding Investors: Not applicable (subsidiary of CATL).
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical process using proprietary DRT (Directional Recycling Technology) which includes intelligent disassembly and material restoration.
Target Materials: Nickel, Cobalt, Manganese, and Lithium. Also produces cathode and anode materials.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap and end-of-life batteries, aiming for a full life-cycle closed-loop system.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations. Processed over 120,000 tonnes of used batteries in 2024. Operates multiple production bases in China (Foshan, Yichang) and is building facilities in Indonesia and other global locations.
Customers Partnerships: Strategic partnerships with Mercedes-Benz and Volkswagen Group. As a subsidiary of CATL, it is deeply integrated into CATL's manufacturing and supply chain.
Geographic Presence: Primarily China, with facilities in Foshan and Yichang. Expanding globally with projects in Indonesia and partnerships aimed at Europe.

Corporate Structure: Public company (Shenzhen Stock Exchange: 002340).
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Investors from early funding rounds include Cowin Capital and Infotech Ventures.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical extraction and processing technologies. Also utilizes directional recycling systems.
Target Materials: Nickel, Cobalt, Lithium, Tungsten, and Manganese. Recovers over 90% of lithium from used batteries.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap (directional recycling with battery plants) and end-of-life batteries from EVs and electronics.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations. Recycled 23,000 tons of power batteries in one year. Operates multiple industrial parks in China, South Korea, and Indonesia, with a total dismantling capacity of 450,000 sets per year.
Customers Partnerships: Has partnerships with over 650 companies, including Mercedes-Benz, Toyota, CATL, Samsung, and ECOPRO BM.
Geographic Presence: Global operations with recycling parks in China, South Korea, Indonesia, and South Africa. Actively deploying in Europe and Southeast Asia.

Corporate Structure: Public company (Shanghai Stock Exchange: 603799). Operates a recycling subsidiary, Huayou Recycling Technology.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company.
Technology Platform: Integrated industrial chain including hydrometallurgical processing and direct recycling.
Target Materials: Primarily Cobalt, Nickel, and Lithium.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap (from partners like LGES Nanjing) and end-of-life EV batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations. Operates multiple recycling plants in China and is building new facilities through joint ventures, such as the plants with LGES in Nanjing and Quzhou, expected to start production in late 2024.
Customers Partnerships: Forms joint ventures and partnerships with major automotive and battery manufacturers, including Ford, Volkswagen, LG Energy Solution, LG Chem, and POSCO.
Geographic Presence: Global operations. Headquarters and multiple plants in China (Zhejiang, Guangxi, Jiangsu). International presence in Indonesia, Democratic Republic of the Congo, and partnerships in Japan, South Korea, and Europe.

Corporate Structure: Public company (listed on KOSDAQ).
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Has received private funding from investors including BNW Investment and EcoPro Partners. Raised KRW 20 billion in a recent funding round.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical "wet process" using solvent extraction. The process involves pre-treatment to create "black powder" followed by chemical extraction. Holds patents related to lithium recovery.
Target Materials: Nickel, Cobalt, Lithium, Manganese, and Copper.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap (scrubs and defective batteries from production lines) and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations with multiple "recycling parks" globally. Has a recycling factory in Hungary and is building new facilities in the US (Georgia, Indiana), Spain, and Germany.
Customers Partnerships: Key customers include Samsung SDI and LG Energy Solution. Also supplies materials to EcoPro EM (a joint venture of Samsung SDI and EcoPro BM). Samsung SDI is also a shareholder (8.57%).
Geographic Presence: Global presence with facilities in South Korea, Hungary, Malaysia, China, and India. Expanding into the United States (Georgia and Indiana) and other parts of Europe (Spain, Germany).

Headquarters: Xinyu, Jiangxi, China. Also lists offices in Shanghai and Toronto.
Corporate Structure: Public company (listed on both Shenzhen and Hong Kong stock exchanges).
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company.
Technology Platform: Integrated model covering lithium resource development, refining, processing, battery manufacturing, and recycling. Developing technologies for solid-state batteries.
Target Materials: Primarily Lithium. Produces a range of lithium products including lithium carbonate, lithium hydroxide, and solid-state lithium batteries.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap and end-of-life recycling as part of its integrated business model.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale. A leading global lithium salt producer that has expanded into lithium battery manufacturing and recycling. 2023 production of lithium batteries and energy storage products reached 10.6 GWh.
Customers Partnerships: Strategic partnership with Turkish battery maker Yigit Aku to establish a joint venture in Turkey.
Geographic Presence: Global. Headquartered in China with operations and investments worldwide, including a new joint venture in Turkey.

Founding Year: 2019 (Revolt program launched). Northvolt was founded in 2016.
Corporate Structure: In-house recycling program and subsidiary of Northvolt AB, a major European battery manufacturer. Operates a joint venture, Hydrovolt, with Norsk Hydro.
Funding Investors: Funded by parent company Northvolt and partners like EIT InnoEnergy. Northvolt has secured over $50 billion in contracts and significant loan financing from institutions like the Nordic Investment Bank (NIB).
Technology Platform: Fully integrated hydrometallurgical process, including mechanical pre-processing (discharging, dismantling) to produce black mass, followed by hydromet treatment.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Manganese, Cobalt.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap (electrode trimmings, reject cells) and end-of-life batteries from EVs and energy storage systems.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale. Operates Europe's largest battery recycling plant, Revolt Ett, alongside the Northvolt Ett gigafactory with a planned capacity of 125,000 tons/year. A pilot plant has been operational since 2020, and a dismantling facility in Hamburg, Germany, since 2023.
Customers Partnerships: Co-located with the Northvolt Ett gigafactory, providing a closed-loop recycling solution for production scrap. Northvolt's key customers include BMW, Volkswagen, Volvo Cars, and Polestar. Joint venture with Norsk Hydro (Hydrovolt).
Geographic Presence: Europe, with major facilities in Sweden (Skellefteå, Västerås) and Germany (Hamburg). Also operates the Hydrovolt JV in Norway.

Corporate Structure: Publicly listed global chemical company (Frankfurt: BAS) with a dedicated Battery Materials and Recycling business unit.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Battery recycling projects are supported by corporate investments and government grants, including grants from the European Commission and the Spanish government.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical process. The company is establishing a full recycling value chain, starting with black mass production from batteries, followed by a hydrometallurgical refinery to recover valuable metals.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, Manganese.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap from gigafactories and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial and pilot operations. Operates a commercial-scale black mass production plant in Schwarzheide, Germany with a capacity of 15,000 tons/year. A prototype metal refinery for processing black mass is also operational at the same site.
Customers Partnerships: Has agreements and partnerships with major players across the value chain, including CATL, Porsche, and Gotion. Also partners with recycling companies like Stena Recycling and ABTC and automotive companies like Iveco Group.
Geographic Presence: Global operations. The battery recycling business is centered in Europe (Schwarzheide, Germany and a planned refinery in Tarragona, Spain) with partnerships and activities in North America and Asia.

Corporate Structure: Publicly traded multinational commodity trading and mining company (LON: GLEN). Has a significant battery recycling business, notably through the acquisition of Li-Cycle.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Invests strategically in recycling companies, with over $300 million invested in Li-Cycle.
Technology Platform: Operates large-scale pyrometallurgical and hydrometallurgical facilities for primary and secondary metals. In battery recycling, it leverages Li-Cycle's "Spoke & Hub" model (mechanical shredding and hydrometallurgical refining).
Target Materials: Nickel and Cobalt are primary focuses. Also involved with Lithium and Manganese through its recycling partnerships and offtake agreements.
Waste Stream Focus: Both primary raw materials from mining and secondary materials from recycling, including battery manufacturing scrap and end-of-life batteries (via Li-Cycle partnership).
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale. Operates numerous metal recycling facilities worldwide. In battery recycling, it is integrating Li-Cycle's "Spoke" facilities and co-developing "Hub" refineries, such as the planned facility in Sardinia, Italy, with a capacity of 50,000-70,000 tonnes of black mass per year.
Customers Partnerships: Strategic partner and majority owner of Li-Cycle. Also has offtake agreements and supplies battery feedstock to various players in the EV supply chain.
Geographic Presence: Global, with operations on every continent. Battery recycling activities are prominent in North America and Europe, including a planned hub in Italy.

Corporate Structure: Publicly traded French mining and metallurgical group.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company, which raises capital through corporate financing such as sustainability-linked bonds.
Technology Platform: Primarily focused on mining and metallurgy (pyrometallurgy and hydrometallurgy). For battery recycling, the company was developing a hydrometallurgical refining process for black mass before the project was suspended. It also has an in-house Direct Lithium Extraction (DLE) technology.
Target Materials: Nickel, Cobalt, Lithium, and Manganese.
Waste Stream Focus: The suspended project focused on both end-of-life batteries and manufacturing scrap.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations in mining and metallurgy. For battery recycling, a demonstration plant was launched in 2023 in Trappes, France, to test a hydrometallurgical process at a pre-industrial scale. However, the battery recycling project was suspended in October 2024. Commercial DLE lithium production started in Argentina.
Customers Partnerships: Has a partnership with Suez for its battery recycling project (ReLieVe) and a partnership with Tsingshan for its lithium project in Argentina. It supplies materials to various industrial manufacturers, including the battery industry.
Geographic Presence: Global presence with operations in approximately 20 countries, including Europe (France, Norway), the Americas (USA, Argentina), Asia (Indonesia), and Africa (Gabon).

Headquarters: Hilchenbach, Germany. Also lists an address in Mönchengladbach, Germany.
Corporate Structure: A joint venture between Australian company Neometals and German engineering firm SMS group. It is now a wholly-owned subsidiary of SMS group.
Funding Investors: Originally funded by JV partners Neometals and SMS group. Now wholly owned by SMS group.
Technology Platform: Integrated mechanical-hydrometallurgical process. This involves discharging, dismantling, and shredding batteries to produce black mass, which is then refined using a hydrometallurgical process.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, Manganese, and Copper.
Waste Stream Focus: End-of-life and scrap batteries from electric vehicles and consumer electronics.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale. The technology is deployed at the Mercedes-Benz plant in Kuppenheim, which has an annual capacity of 2,500 tonnes and began operations in 2024. Primobius is transitioning from pilot to deploying industrial-scale plants globally.
Customers Partnerships: Technology partner for Mercedes-Benz's battery recycling factory in Kuppenheim, Germany. Continues joint technology development with former partner Neometals.
Geographic Presence: Headquartered in Germany with plans for global roll-out of its recycling plants, targeting North America.

Corporate Structure: Venture-backed startup / scale-up (Series A). A spin-off from RWTH Aachen University.
Funding Investors: Total funding secured is over €100 million, including a €55 million Series A round and over €27 million in grants. Key investors include World Fund, Porsche Ventures, and Bosch Ventures.
Technology Platform: Proprietary, water-based hydrometallurgical end-to-end process (OLiC - Optimised Lithium & Graphite Recovery) developed at RWTH Aachen University.
Target Materials: Lithium, Graphite, Nickel, Cobalt, and Manganese from both NMC and LFP battery chemistries.
Waste Stream Focus: Processes all types of lithium-ion battery waste, including end-of-life battery packs, manufacturing production waste, and black mass.
Deployment Stage: Pilot and scaling to commercial. Operates a pilot line in Aachen, Germany. Currently building its first industrial-scale plant in Dormagen, Germany, scheduled to begin operations in 2027 with a capacity to recycle up to 30,000 tons of batteries annually.
Customers Partnerships: Has completed projects for major automotive OEMs and battery manufacturers. Named partners include Webasto, Pure Battery Technologies (MOU), and Syensqo. Porsche and Bosch are key investors.
Geographic Presence: Europe, with a focus on Germany (Aachen and Dormagen).

Corporate Structure: Initially a 50:50 joint venture between Norsk Hydro and Northvolt. As of early 2025, it is a wholly owned subsidiary of Norsk Hydro.
Funding Investors: Financed by parent company Norsk Hydro. Also received 63 million NOK from DNB and Eksfin, and 15.3 million NOK from Enova.
Technology Platform: Mechanical recycling process involving automated discharging, dismantling, and shredding to produce black mass and other materials. The black mass is then supplied to partners like Northvolt for hydrometallurgical refining.
Target Materials: Recovers black mass containing Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, and Manganese, as well as aluminum, copper, and plastics.
Waste Stream Focus: End-of-life batteries from electric vehicles and industrial applications.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations. Operates one of Europe's largest EV battery recycling plants in Fredrikstad, Norway, with a capacity of 12,000 tons of battery packs per year. A new plant is under construction in Hordain, France. The company aims to recycle 300,000 tons of battery packs by 2030.
Customers Partnerships: Maintains a commercial collaboration with Northvolt as a buyer of its black mass.
Geographic Presence: Europe. Current operations are in Norway (Oslo and Fredrikstad), with expansion underway in France (Hordain) and plans for further facilities in Europe.

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed startup (Seed stage).
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is €17 million ($17.9 million), including an €11 million seed round and a €2.5 million grant from the European Innovation Council. The seed round was led by NordicNinja with participation from In-Q-Tel, Honda, JGC Group, Atlantic Labs, and Verve Ventures.
Technology Platform: Proprietary hydrometallurgical process that is described as a low-temperature, low-emission alternative to traditional smelting.
Target Materials: Primarily Lithium and Graphite. Also mentioned are Nickel, Cobalt, and Manganese.
Waste Stream Focus: Recycles various types of battery waste, implying both end-of-life and manufacturing scrap.
Deployment Stage: Pilot plant is operational and has made its first commercial deliveries of recycled lithium. Planning to build its first industrial-scale plant with a target capacity of 30,000 tons/year by 2026.
Customers Partnerships: Completed a pilot project with Circular Republic that involved BMW, MAN, and Webasto. Has expanded partnerships with battery waste suppliers in over 10 countries. Honda is a strategic investor.
Geographic Presence: Europe, headquartered in Germany. Works with suppliers across more than 10 countries. Has collaboration interest from Japan.

Headquarters: Bremerhaven, Germany. Some sources also list Offenbach, Germany.
Corporate Structure: Acquired by and now a subsidiary of Redwood Materials. It was previously part of the Saubermacher group.
Funding Investors: Owned by Redwood Materials. Has previously received grant funding.
Technology Platform: Multi-stage process including mechanical separation and thermal pre-treatment, which is now integrated with Redwood Materials' hydrometallurgical processes.
Target Materials: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, Manganese, and Copper. Achieves a recovery rate of up to 95%.
Waste Stream Focus: Both battery manufacturing waste and end-of-life batteries from EVs, e-bikes, consumer electronics, and stationary storage systems.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations. The Bremerhaven, Germany facility has a processing capacity of 10,000 tonnes per year and is currently being expanded.
Customers Partnerships: As part of Redwood Materials, it serves the European battery supply chain. No specific gigafactory partnerships are named.
Geographic Presence: Europe, with its main facility in Bremerhaven, Germany. Exploring new sites in Central Europe and Scandinavia.

Corporate Structure: Private company (GmbH).
Funding Investors: Has raised funding over 12 rounds, including grants. The total amount is undisclosed.
Technology Platform: Specializes in recycling various battery types including Nickel Cadmium (NiCd), Nickel Metal Hydride (NiMH), and Lithium-ion batteries. The specific process (e.g., hydrometallurgical) is not detailed.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel (from NiCd and NiMH batteries). Other specific materials like Co, Mn, Graphite are not mentioned.
Waste Stream Focus: End-of-life batteries of various types. Manufacturing scrap focus is not explicitly mentioned.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations, with over 20 years of experience in the battery recycling market.
Customers Partnerships: Not mentioned in source.
Geographic Presence: Germany. Described as a global leader, suggesting international business.

Founding Year: 2011. Formed as a Delaware corporation in 2014.
Corporate Structure: Public company (NASDAQ: ABAT).
Funding Investors: Publicly traded. Has received significant federal grants from the U.S. Department of Energy, totaling over $100 million. Also utilizes convertible notes for financing.
Technology Platform: Internally-developed hydrometallurgical and chemical extraction processes, described as a "targeted non-smelting process" and "selective leach extraction (SLE)".
Target Materials: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Manganese. Also produces graphite, copper, and aluminum.
Waste Stream Focus: Both end-of-life batteries and manufacturing scrap from gigafactories and automotive partners. Also developing primary mineral resources.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations. First commercial recycling facility near Reno, Nevada (opened Fall 2023) has a capacity of 20,000 metric tons/year. A second, larger facility (100,000 tonnes/year) is planned, supported by a DOE grant. Also operates a multi-tonne per day pilot plant for primary lithium extraction.
Customers Partnerships: Strategic partnership with BASF for offtake of battery-grade metals. Also collaborates with Siemens, Clemson University (CU-ICAR), and Argonne National Laboratory (ReCell Center). UNR Partnership is also mentioned.
Geographic Presence: United States, with all mentioned facilities and headquarters located in Nevada (Reno, TRIC, Fernley, Tonopah).

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed startup (Series A). A spin-out from Princeton University.
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is over $55 million. This includes a $30 million Series A round and $18 million in grants from the U.S. Department of Energy. Investors include Honda, Samsung Ventures, LKQ Corp, and SCG Group.
Technology Platform: Direct Recycling using a patented low-temperature, plasma-assisted separation process (LPAS™).
Target Materials: Recovers up to 95% of all materials in lithium-ion batteries, producing battery-grade cathode and anode materials. This implies recovery of Li, Co, Ni, Mn, and Graphite.
Waste Stream Focus: Not explicitly stated, but the "direct recycling" and "cathode-to-cathode" approach suggests a focus on both manufacturing scrap and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Transitioning from pilot to commercial scale. The company is using its Series A funding to construct its first standalone, full-scale direct recycling advanced manufacturing facility.
Customers Partnerships: Has strategic investors and partners including Honda Motor Co. Ltd. (MOU), Samsung Venture Investment Corp., LKQ Corporation, and Shell Ventures.
Geographic Presence: United States. Based in New Jersey, with plans to build its first commercial facility in the southeastern U.S. "battery belt".

Corporate Structure: Private company, majority-owned by The Heritage Group. Formed through the merger of several recycling companies.
Funding Investors: Received a $245M minority investment from EQT Infrastructure, a $50M investment led by Marubeni Corporation, and over $200M in federal grants from the U.S. Department of Energy.
Technology Platform: Advanced hydrometallurgical processing to refine black mass into high-purity battery materials.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, and Cobalt. Produces battery-grade metal salts and sulfates.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap from gigafactories and end-of-life batteries from EVs, energy storage, and consumer electronics.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations with multiple facilities across North America. Current capacity is over 60,000 tons/year and is expanding significantly with new and upgraded facilities in South Carolina and Ohio, aiming to process hundreds of thousands of EV batteries annually.
Customers Partnerships: Serves automotive manufacturers, battery producers, and electronics companies. Has partnerships with Jacobs Engineering, Volta Energy Technologies, and KULR.
Geographic Presence: North America, with coast-to-coast coverage in the United States. Facilities are located in Ohio, South Carolina, and Arizona.

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed scale-up (Series A).
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is approximately $120M. Key investors include GM Ventures, Investissement Québec, IMM Investment, and Fondaction.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical process. It is a two-step "spoke and hub" model involving mechanical pre-treatment to create black mass, followed by a hydrometallurgical process to purify the materials.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, Graphite, and other strategic materials. Aims to recover over 95% of materials.
Waste Stream Focus: Both industrial/manufacturing waste ("non-conforming products") and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial. Launched its first commercial recycling plant in 2023 with a capacity of 7,500 metric tons/year. A hydrometallurgical "Hub" plant is planned for 2025. This follows a successful industrial-scale demonstration plant commissioned in 2020.
Customers Partnerships: Has a strategic partnership with General Motors (GM), with GM Ventures as an investor. The collaboration focuses on validating recovered materials for use in new batteries. Also has partnerships with Hyundai Auto Canada and Nissan Canada.
Geographic Presence: Currently based in Canada (Quebec), with plans to expand and build facilities in the United States, Europe, and South Korea.

Headquarters: Dallas/Carrollton, Texas, USA. An address in Aubrey, Texas is also listed.
Corporate Structure: Private, private equity-backed company.
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is $20 million from a private equity round led by Freestone HoldCo (a portfolio company of Tailwater Capital) in 2021. Also has support from DOE and Halliburton Labs.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical - proprietary Membrane Solvent Extraction (MSX) technology, which is a modular and scalable process.
Target Materials: High-purity critical metals and minerals, including rare earth elements. Specific battery metals like Li, Co, Ni are implied but not explicitly listed in all sources.
Waste Stream Focus: Electronic and battery waste, including EV batteries and scrapped permanent magnets. The modular system is offered to gigafactories and recyclers.
Deployment Stage: Demonstration and scaling to commercial. Operates a demonstration unit in Carrollton, Texas, with a capacity of 200 tons per year (TPY). A new commercial-scale facility is planned to open in Ohio. Technology Readiness Level (TRL) is rated at 4-6.
Customers Partnerships: Has technology development partnerships with the U.S. Department of Energy, Oak Ridge National Lab, and Halliburton Labs. Provides services to gigafactories, recyclers, and OEMs.
Geographic Presence: United States. Headquarters and R&D lab are in Texas, with a commercial plant planned for Ohio.

Corporate Structure: Public company (NASDAQ: AQMS). Formed as a Delaware corporation in 2014.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded. Has raised capital through equity raises and convertible notes. Total funding from early rounds was $6M.
Technology Platform: Patented electrochemical / hydrometallurgical process called AquaRefining™, which uses electricity-powered electroplating at room temperature.
Target Materials: Lithium (as lithium carbonate and hydroxide), Cobalt, Nickel, and Manganese.
Waste Stream Focus: Lithium-ion battery black mass from pre-processed end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Pilot and commercial deployment. Operates a pilot facility at its Innovation Center in Reno, Nevada. The first commercial-scale plant (Sierra ARC, Phase 1) with a capacity of 3,000 tons/year of black mass is commissioning in 2024, with plans to expand to 10,000 tons/year.
Customers Partnerships: Has a multi-year supply agreement with 6K Energy and an LOI with Westwin Elements. Has a licensing partner in Taiwan. The Innovation Center is located in the same industrial park as the Tesla Gigafactory.
Geographic Presence: United States (Reno, Nevada) and expanding to the APAC region through a licensed facility in Taiwan.

Corporate Structure: Private technology development company (startup/SME). Seeks partnerships for equity investment, licensing, and tech-transfer.
Funding Investors: Has raised at least $1.5M through various funding rounds, including grants from government agencies like the US DOE and UK Government's OZEV.
Technology Platform: Direct Recycling using patented "Cathode Healing™" and "Inertization" processes, which restore electrode materials for direct reuse and render batteries safe for transport and handling.
Target Materials: Cathode materials, including Lithium Cobalt Oxide (LCO) and Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP). Also recovers electrolyte and critical materials from black mass.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing scrap ("on-site cathode healing" for production waste) and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Pilot plant stage. The company is focused on scaling up its patented processes from lab and pilot scale to industrial demonstrations with partners.
Customers Partnerships: Has active programs and collaborations with the US Department of Energy, Defense Logistics Agency, Johnson Matthey, and the UK Battery Industrialisation Centre (UKBIC).
Geographic Presence: United States (headquarters) and United Kingdom (collaboration with UKBIC).

Corporate Structure: A division of the private, venture-backed scale-up company 6K Inc..
Funding Investors: Parent company 6K has raised over $200M, including a $102M Series D round and an $82M Series E round. Investors include Koch Strategic Platforms, Stellantis, Albemarle, Anzu Partners, and Energy Impact Partners. Received a $50M grant from the U.S. DOE.
Technology Platform: Proprietary UniMelt® microwave plasma technology, an advanced thermal process that produces materials directly from feedstock, replacing traditional hydrometallurgical co-precipitation methods.
Target Materials: Cathode Active Materials (CAM) for both NMC (Nickel Manganese Cobalt) and LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate) chemistries.
Waste Stream Focus: Utilizes recycled content from partners to create a circular supply chain, implying both manufacturing scrap and end-of-life feedstocks.
Deployment Stage: Pilot to commercial scale. Operates a Battery Center of Excellence with a capacity of up to 1 ton/month of CAM for customer qualification. A full-scale commercial plant (PlusCAM) is under construction in Jackson, TN, scheduled to open in late 2024 with a 13,000 tonnes/year capacity.
Customers Partnerships: Has a strategic partnership and investment from Stellantis. Also has partnerships with Albemarle and Heritage/Retriev. Currently negotiating offtake agreements with major automotive OEMs.
Geographic Presence: United States. Facilities are located in Massachusetts (HQ), Pennsylvania, and Tennessee.

Corporate Structure: A 65:35 joint venture between South Korean conglomerate POSCO and Chinese company Huayou Cobalt. It is a subsidiary of POSCO Group.
Funding Investors: Funded by the joint venture partners, with POSCO investing 120 billion won ($107.6 million) for the initial facility.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical and/or pyrometallurgical process to extract cathode materials from "black powder". The process is described as "melting and refining".
Target Materials: Nickel, Lithium, Cobalt, and Manganese.
Waste Stream Focus: Processes "black powder" derived from waste battery scraps imported from Europe. This implies a focus on both end-of-life and manufacturing scrap that has been pre-processed.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale. The plant, with a capacity of about 20,000 tons, was completed in July 2023 and reached a 92% operation rate by June 2024. An earlier report mentioned a planned capacity of 10,000 tons.
Customers Partnerships: Joint venture partnership with Huayou Cobalt. Currently undergoing quality certification with POSCO Future M and other local cathode and electrolyte manufacturers in South Korea.
Geographic Presence: South Korea. The recycling plant is located in the Yulchon Industrial Complex in Gwangyang. Feedstock is imported from Europe.

Corporate Structure: A major Chinese public company (SHA: 688778) specializing in battery materials.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Funding is through corporate investments and joint ventures. The French JV with Orano represents a €1.5 billion investment.
Technology Platform: Primarily a manufacturer of cathode active materials (CAM) and precursors (PCAM) using chemical synthesis processes. Not a recycling company, but a downstream partner for recycled materials.
Target Materials: Lithium Cobalt Oxide (LCO) and Nickel-Cobalt-Manganese (NCM) ternary cathode materials. This implies a focus on Li, Co, Ni, and Mn.
Waste Stream Focus: Not applicable (materials producer, not a recycler).
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale manufacturer with 8 existing production sites in China. Currently expanding with a new high-end energy material project in China and the large-scale JV project in Dunkirk, France.
Customers Partnerships: Has a major joint venture with French nuclear fuel company Orano to build CAM and PCAM plants in France. Also has a strategic cooperation agreement with Xinwangda for solid-state batteries. The French facility aims to secure orders from European gigafactories.
Geographic Presence: Worldwide. Headquartered in China with 8 production sites and multiple subsidiaries. Expanding into Europe with a major manufacturing hub in Dunkirk, France. Has a European subsidiary, XTC New Energy Materials Europe GmbH.

Corporate Structure: Publicly listed company in China (SHA: 688148). Operates recycling through subsidiaries Jiangmen Fangyuan Recycling and Jiangmen Fangyuan Lithium Technology.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Planned to raise RMB 1.886 billion for its recycling project. Early-stage institutional investors included Jinbang Capital and Harvest Capital.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical ("wet-process metallurgy") for recycling and materials production.
Target Materials: Primarily Lithium, Nickel, and Cobalt for ternary precursors (NCA and NCM). The new recycling project focuses on Lithium and Iron Phosphate from LFP batteries.
Waste Stream Focus: Focusing on recycling of scrap and end-of-life Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) batteries. The company also has a battery dismantling business.
Deployment Stage: Commercial manufacturer of cathode materials. Is developing battery recycling capabilities, with a planned facility in Jiangmen to process 300,000 tons/year of LFP batteries and produce 80,000 tons/year of cathode materials. A project for comprehensive utilization of scrap LFP batteries was recently terminated.
Customers Partnerships: Has a letter of intention with a major Chinese carmaker for collaboration on lithium carbonate. Owns a 20% stake in a joint venture with Veolia (Veolia New Energy Technology Jiangmen).
Geographic Presence: China, with operations in Guangdong province (Jiangmen). Distributes products to domestic and overseas markets.

Corporate Structure: The environment and energy arm of SK Group, a major South Korean conglomerate. Operates as a construction engineering and waste management company.
Funding Investors: As a subsidiary of SK Group, funding is primarily corporate. It strategically invests in other companies, having previously held a significant stake in Ascend Elements.
Technology Platform: Hydrometallurgical solvent extraction methods to achieve high recovery rates (97% for Ni and Co). Also uses mechanical pre-processing (dismantling and shredding) to produce black mass in its joint venture.
Target Materials: Nickel and Cobalt from spent lithium-ion batteries are a primary focus. The company has also developed technology to produce Lithium Hydroxide.
Waste Stream Focus: Both spent/end-of-life batteries and gigafactory manufacturing scrap.
Deployment Stage: Commercial and pilot operations. Has developed four core battery-recycling technologies that it plans to commercialize in pilot plants and apply to its full-scale battery recycling plant in Gyeongju, South Korea, scheduled for completion in 2025. The US joint venture plant in Kentucky is under construction (2023-2025) and will process 24,000 metric tons/year of scrap.
Customers Partnerships: Has a joint venture with Ascend Elements and its own subsidiary TES to build a recycling facility in Kentucky. Has academic collaborations with several Korean universities and institutes.
Geographic Presence: Global. Headquartered in South Korea, with facilities and joint ventures in the US (Kentucky, Virginia), and a presence in Europe and Asia. Also planning a facility in Jiangsu, China.

Headquarters: Australia (implied). Not explicitly stated in sources.
Corporate Structure: Publicly listed Australian company (ASX) focused on battery materials technology. Commercializes its technology through joint ventures and licensing.
Funding Investors: Publicly traded company. Recently raised A$9 million from institutional investors.
Technology Platform: Develops and licenses multiple processing technologies. For battery recycling, it is commercializing the mechanical-hydrometallurgical process via its Primobius JV. For lithium, it has developed the ELi™ Process, an electrolysis technology to produce lithium hydroxide.
Target Materials: Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt, and Vanadium.
Waste Stream Focus: Both industrial waste streams and end-of-life lithium-ion batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercialization via partners. The Primobius JV provides commercial recycling services in Germany and is licensing its technology in North America. Neometals' proprietary ELi Process is at the pilot/demonstration plant stage (2023-2024).
Customers Partnerships: Key partnership is the Primobius joint venture with SMS group, which has Mercedes-Benz as its first major partner. Also has a planned JV with Bondalti in Portugal for lithium chemicals.
Geographic Presence: Australia (home base), Europe (Germany, Portugal), and targeting North America (Canada).

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed company (Series D). Does business as ElectraMet, with the legal entity being PowerTech Water.
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is $25.39M. Investors include Veriten's NexTen fund, HG Ventures, EnerTech Capital, Scale Venture Partners, the U.S. Department of Energy, and Wieland.
Technology Platform: Electrochemical process using patented carbon electrode technology to selectively remove and recover dissolved metals from water and process streams without chemicals.
Target Materials: Primarily focused on removing impurity metals like Copper and Manganese from process streams. Also addresses Cobalt. Its technology is applied to purify Lithium, Cobalt, and Nickel streams.
Waste Stream Focus: Industrial process and wastewater streams, including from EV battery recycling (black mass refining) and semiconductor manufacturing.
Deployment Stage: Commercial deployment. The company offers modular, automated systems and provides services from feasibility testing to full-scale implementation.
Customers Partnerships: Has a partnership with Xylem to provide an integrated solution for metals recovery. Wieland, a copper specialist, is a strategic investor. Serves global industry leaders.
Geographic Presence: Based in the United States (Kentucky) with plans to expand operations into Asia and Europe.

Corporate Structure: Private, seed-stage startup. Some sources list it as a public company, which may be inaccurate given the seed stage.
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is between $1.52M and $5.8M (sources vary). The seed round was led by Diniz Holding and SABIC Ventures, with participation from APY Ventures and others.
Technology Platform: Non-chemical Direct Recycling. The process involves direct disassembly of batteries without shredding to recover materials at high purity, avoiding the creation of black mass.
Target Materials: Graphite, Lithium, and Cobalt are explicitly mentioned. The technology aims to recover up to 96% of all battery materials.
Waste Stream Focus: Both manufacturing production scrap and end-of-life batteries.
Deployment Stage: Lab-scale validation and preparing for industrialization. The company is poised to move from lab trials to larger-scale deployment.
Customers Partnerships: Not mentioned in source.
Geographic Presence: United States (Sunnyvale, CA) and Turkey (incubation facility). Also has a focus on the European market.

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed startup (Seed stage). A spin-off from MIT.
Funding Investors: Raised $11.8 million in a seed funding round led by 2150. Total funding is $17.73M. Investors include BHP Ventures, Extantia, Orion Industrial Ventures, Azolla Ventures, and the E14 Fund.
Technology Platform: Patented porous silicon membrane technology that combines electro-filtration and electro-extraction for chemical-free, energy-efficient recovery of metals.
Target Materials: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Copper.
Waste Stream Focus: Both lithium-ion battery recycling streams (including black mass) and mining waste streams (e.g., heap leachate, tailings).
Deployment Stage: Pilot stage. The company is using its seed funding to deploy pilot systems with its commercial partners to scale the technology.
Customers Partnerships: Has a partnership with mining company Rio Tinto. BHP is also a strategic investor/partner.
Geographic Presence: United States (based in Boston/Cambridge, Massachusetts). Aims for global deployment with mining partners.

Founding Year: 2017 (rebranded from American Rare Earth, created in 2020).
Corporate Structure: A portfolio company and subsidiary of American Resources Corporation (NASDAQ:AREC), with plans to be spun-off as a separate entity.
Funding Investors: Has raised over $500M. Received a $200M equity facility from Transition Equity Partners and is in process for a potential $700M conditional loan from the U.S. Department of Defense. Investors include various government and private equity firms.
Technology Platform: Proprietary chromatographic separation and refining technology licensed from Purdue University. It is a modular, multi-feedstock platform that can be deployed on-site.
Target Materials: Rare Earth Elements (REEs), Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, Manganese, Antimony, Gallium, and Germanium. Produces battery-grade cathode materials (hydroxides, carbonates, sulfates).
Waste Stream Focus: Processes a wide variety of feedstocks, including recycled materials from rare earth magnets and lithium-ion batteries, manufacturing byproducts, and primary ore concentrates.
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations. Operates a commercialization facility in Noblesville, Indiana, and is scaling up its first commercial-scale refining site in Marion, Indiana, with a target capacity of over 10,000 metric tons annually. Operational deployment is targeted for Q2 2026.
Customers Partnerships: Has collaborations with POSCO International and a partnership with Vulcan Elements for vertical integration into magnet production. Partners with feedstock providers and downstream customers.
Geographic Presence: United States (Indiana). Has international operations and is expanding to Africa (Ghana) and other allied jurisdictions.

Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed scale-up (Series B).
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is over $120 million. Secured a $54 million Series B round in 2024 from investors including Singularity Growth, Baring Private Equity, Cactus Venture Partners, and Venture East.
Technology Platform: Integrated, proprietary technology for battery recycling, raw material refining (low-carbon), and battery repurposing (second-life applications).
Target Materials: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, Manganese, and Carbon/Graphite. Produces Pre-CAM and CAM materials.
Waste Stream Focus: Focuses on the entire battery lifecycle, including recycling end-of-life batteries and repurposing used batteries for second-life energy storage systems (ESS).
Deployment Stage: Commercial operations. Described as India's largest producer of sustainable energy transition materials, with significant revenue and a workforce of around 600. Currently scaling up capacity.
Customers Partnerships: Has a joint venture with the UAE's Ministry of Energy & Infrastructure and BEEAH Group. Also partners with ReElement Technologies and Stride Green.
Geographic Presence: India (headquartered in Delhi/Noida). Expanding internationally to North America, the EU, the Middle East, and Africa. Has a partnership in the UAE.

Headquarters: Noida/Roorkee/Dehradun, India (sources vary on primary HQ)
Corporate Structure: Private, venture-backed company (Series C). Legal entity is Attero Recycling Private Limited.
Funding Investors: Total funding raised is $31.1 million. Lead investors include NEA-Indo US Venture, DFJ, IFC, and Kalaari Capital. Last funding round was a Series C in 2014.
Technology Platform: Patented, proprietary technologies for e-waste and lithium-ion battery recycling, focused on metal extraction. Claims an extraction efficiency of 98%. The specific method is not detailed but is implied to be a form of hydrometallurgy or chemical extraction.
Target Materials: Cobalt, Nickel, Lithium, Graphite, and Manganese, in addition to other precious metals from e-waste.
Waste Stream Focus: End-of-life electronic waste (e-waste) and lithium-ion batteries.
Deployment Stage: Commercial-scale operations. Operates one of India's largest integrated recycling plants in Roorkee with a capacity of 144,000 tonnes/year for e-waste and 15,000 tonnes/year for lithium-ion batteries. Planning to expand total capacity to 244,000 tonnes/year with new greenfield facilities.
Customers Partnerships: Provides support to OEMs for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) compliance.
Geographic Presence: India (Roorkee, Noida, Dehradun). Expanding to Europe with a new facility under construction in Poland.
